get.description:
Returns the most recently created session with
"active" status for the authenticated user.
Returns null data if no active session exists.
get.summary: Get the currently active audit session

get.description:
Paginated list of sessions with their associated OLT.
Supports `?per_page=` to control page size.
get.summary: List all audit sessions for the authenticated user

post.description:
Links an OLT to a named session for tracking ONU audits.
The session name auto-generates as AUDIT-YYYYMMDD-XXX
if not provided. Starts in "active" status.

get.description:
Returns the session along with its permanently saved
ONUs, temporary saved ONUs, and associated OLT.
Only the session owner can view it.
get.summary: Get audit session details with ONUs

delete.description:
Permanently removes the session and all associated
ONU records. Only the session owner can delete it.

post.description:
Persists scanned ONU data to the session's onus table.
Session must be owned by the authenticated user and
must have "active" status.
post.summary: Save ONUs permanently to an audit session

post.description:
Sets the session status to "completed" and records
the completion timestamp. Only the session owner
can complete it.
post.summary: Mark an audit session as completed

get.description:
Returns all ONUs currently stored in the session's
temporary storage (saved_onus table).

post.description:
Stores ONU data in the saved_onus table (survives
page refresh). Only inserts ONUs whose SN does not
already exist. Returns count of added and total records.
post.summary: Save ONUs temporarily to an audit session

delete.description:
Removes every ONU from the session's temporary
storage in a single operation.

Deletes one ONU from the session's temporary storage
identified by its SN. Returns the remaining count.
delete.summary: Remove a single temporary ONU by serial number

post.description:
Validates email and password credentials. On success returns a
Bearer token and the authenticated user's basic profile.
On failure returns a 422 validation error.
post.summary: Authenticate a user and issue a Sanctum token

post.description:
Invalidates the Bearer token used in the request. Subsequent
requests with the same token will be rejected.

get.description:
Returns basic user information (id, name, email) for the
currently authenticated user.

Returns every OLT record. Passwords are always excluded
from the response (encrypted at rest).

post.description:
Stores connection credentials. The password is automatically
encrypted using Crypt::encryptString() before persisting.
post.summary: Create a new OLT device record

get.description: Returns the OLT record identified by the given ID.
get.summary: Get a single OLT device details

put.description:
All fields are optional. If password is empty or null,
the existing encrypted password is preserved.

delete.description:
Permanently removes the OLT and all associated ONUs,
history entries, and audit sessions.

get.description:
Paginated list of all OLT command executions.
Supports `?filter=daily` for today's records or
`?filter=monthly` for this month. Defaults to all.
Use `?per_page=` to control page size.

delete.description:
Deletes history entries filtered by `?filter=daily`
or `?filter=monthly`. Without a filter, all records
are deleted. Returns the count of removed records.

get.description:
Streams a CSV file with columns: Date, User, OLT,
Action, Target SN, Command, Status. Supports
`?filter=daily` or `?filter=monthly`.

Returns a key-value map of all preferences for the
authenticated user. Values stored as JSON are
automatically decoded in the response.

put.description:
Accepts a single key-value pair or a batch of
preferences. For batch updates, pass an object
of key-value pairs in the `batch` field.
Missing keys are not affected.

post.description:
Connects via Telnet, runs `show pon onu u`, and parses
the output into structured ONU data including olt_index,
model, serial number, and password.
post.summary: Scan an OLT for unconfigured ONUs

post.description:
Captures the initial banner text displayed by the OLT
before the login prompt, useful for verifying connectivity
and device identity.

Sends a command via Telnet and returns the raw output.
Command safety restrictions apply: max 500 chars, blocks
shell metacharacters (;, &&, ||, |, backticks, $()) and
destructive commands (rm, del, format).
post.summary: Execute an arbitrary command on an OLT

post.description:
Runs `show gpon onu detail-info` for the given olt_index
and returns parsed data including state, signal levels,
firmware version, and configuration profiles.
post.summary: Get detailed information about a specific ONU

post.description:
Accepts connection details directly without requiring a
pre-existing OLT record. If an OLT with the same host
already exists, it reuses it. Otherwise, a new OLT record
named "Quick Scan ({host})" is created automatically.
post.summary: Quick scan an OLT using inline credentials

get.description:
Returns credential presets. Passwords are not included
in the response (stored in plaintext).

post.description:
Stores a credential preset for quick-scan flows.
Unlike OLT records, template passwords are stored
in plaintext.
post.summary: Create a new OLT connection template

put.description:
All fields are optional. Empty password preserves
the existing value.

patch.description:
If the template is already the default, removes its
default status. Otherwise, clears all templates'
is_default flags and sets this one.
patch.summary: Toggle a template as the default

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
